item.title: The Books I Read in 2018
item.description: Early in the year I set out to read 24 books (two per month) in 2018. I actually blew past 24 books fairly quickly, in part due to Andrzej Sapkowski’s “Witcher” series books1 which are pretty quick reads, so I changed my goal to 36 books (three per …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Gartner IAM Summit - Day 0
item.description: I’m in Las Vegas this week for Gartner’s IAM Summit, being held at Ceasar’s Palace. Vegas isn’t really my cup of tea but one goes where the conferences are. Pro tip: The desert is really freaking cold in December. I mean, not Canada cold, but way …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 13
item.description:
Salaberry-de-Valleyfield

Distance traveled: 240kms

Home Sweet Home
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 12
item.description:
Edmundston to Salaberry-de-Valleyfield

Distance traveled (approximate): 611kms

Almost homeward bound. More of those gorgeous inter-New Brunswick - Quebec views.

On our way to our last motel, we stopped at the massive Carrier Harley / Can-Am / BMW Motorrad shop in Drummondville. These guys had everything, even the BMW Motorrad bikini, flip-flops, and board …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 11
item.description:
Antigonish to Moncton to Edmundston.

Distacnce traveled (approximate): 718kms

Today was a very long haul, with a somewhat unplanned detour. The Harley was very clearly spitting oil out the right side so on our way to Edmunstun, we headed into Moncton to visit Toys for Big Boys Harley-Davidson. Apparently the …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 10
item.description: Channel-Port aux Basques to Antigonish. We altered our return ferry to cross in the daytime. We also got lucky and we able to upgrade to a berth so we could at least try to nap. Still not the best way to do the crossing on motorcycles since you are still …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 9
item.description:
From the Lodge to Port aux Basques.

Distance traveled (approximate): 528kms

And we’re off! We’ve made some adjustments to our return plans. We will spend the night in Channel-Port aux Basques and then take a day crossing back to North Sydney. Ideally we would then spend a night …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 8
item.description: An honest-to-goodness day off. Well, sort of. We start our trip back home tomorrow so there was all the packing and pre-ride checks to do. Still, mostly a day of patio sitting!
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 7
item.description:
From the Lodge to Mount Pearl and back!

Distance traveled (approximate): 734kms

It’s Pick Up the Harley Day! My buddy DreadPirateRoberts and his Father-in-Law took the four-wheeled cage and I followed along on my Beemer. It’s a long haul to St. John’s / Mount Pearl and back but …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 6
item.description: With the Harley in the shop getting fixed up we took one of those four wheeled vehicular contraptions to do some touring. We took the 340 North to the Beothuk Interpretation Centre (the Beothuk were the indigenous population in Newfoundland). The picture below is the waterfront at the Interpretaion Centre …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 5
item.description: With my buddy’s bike broken down, and us waiting on Rugged Rock Harley-Davidson to come pick it up, I headed out for a little ride. I went to Gander, and then took the 330 North to Musgrave Harbour. I had originally planned to take the 330 all the way …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 4
item.description:
Channel-Port aux Basques to the Lodge

Distance traveled (approximate): 529kms

Pulling into Channel-Port aux Basques.

It turned out to be a pretty eventful day. The weather when we got off the ferry was terrible. Heavy rain and transport trucks on the Trans-Canada Highway lead to lots of spray and dicey …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 3
item.description:
Moncton to the North Sydney Ferry Terminal.

Distance traveled (approximate): 481kms

After getting to Cape Breton, about half way to North Sydney is Big Spruce Brewing, a small local brewery high on a hill overlooking the water. Well worth the visit. I don’t really have a decent picture that …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 2
item.description:
Saint-Jean-Port-Joli to Moncton

Distance traveled (approximate): 640kms

From Saint-Jean-Port-Joli we made our way to Moncton. The ride between Quebec City area all the way into New Brunswick is gorgeous. I kinda regret not taking my GoPro, but video logging a motorcycle trip is a pile of work. This first picture …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: The Viking Tour - Day 1
item.description:
Home to Saint-Jean-Port-Joli

Distance traveled (approximate): 635kms

So once a year a buddy and I, along with his wife, head off on one long motorcycle trip. Last year was “The Moose Tour” to Bar Harbour, Maine, with another riding buddy in tow. This year it’s to be The Viking …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: I Love New Tires (and Routine Maintenance)
item.description: Closing in on 20,000kms on my 2016 R1200GS, and with my moto trip to Newfoundland looming, I booked the bike in for its 20,000km service. I really enjoy taking my vehicles in for routine maintenance. It’s surprisingly satisfying. Even more satisfying is new tires. I just loving …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: Forking Seals
item.description: The quick fix on the left fork seal did not work. Not long after leaving the shop you could see fork oil slowly accumulating on top of the seal and on the fork itself. I called Endras back and they booked my in for Friday the 28th. Hopefully last minute …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: Cloud and Identity Systems Lead
item.description: A new shift in my responsibilities at work, with more focus on two key aspects of our environment that I have been leading on the operations side of things: Consolidating our cloud computing resources on Google Cloud Platform and our migration to our new Identity as a Service partner, Auth0 …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: Linux Foundation Certified Engineer
item.description: Renewal time for my LFCS certification. The Linux Foundation will renew your LFCS certification for 2 years if you upgrade to the LFCE cert, so that’s what I did!
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: I Broke My GS - Update
item.description: One of the service reps at my dealership went to bat for me and got me squared away. My GS will be all fixed up in plenty of time for my trip to Maine.
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: I Broke My GS
item.description:
I broke my GS.

No injuries other than my pride. Less than a second’s worth of inattention and I dropped my bike at a gas station while I was out joy riding this past Sunday (July 16th). The resulting chaos was impressive for such a silly little tip-over.

I …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: And My Axe!
item.description:
I bought a guitar: An Epiphone Les Paul Standard PlusTop Pro in “Bourbon Burst” colour scheme. And it is freakin’ gorgeous.

I’ve started hacking on some basics, chords and chord transitions, various forms of scales, and lots of YouTube lessons (Lessons with Cameron, Andy Guitar, and others). Nothing formal …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: My 2016 BMW R1200GS
item.description:
Like all right thinking peoples, I like to ride the train. And I particularly like riding the train when there’s a brand new motorcycle waiting for me at the other end of the line.

Of course, a good book is a great way to pass the time if the …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
item.description: Another certification in the bag. I wrote the AWS Associate level Solutions Architect exam today. I didn’t realize AWS certs are only good for 2 years. Sets a bit of a roadmap for my AWS certifications going forward. I got my Associate level SysOps cert in earl 2015, so …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: A New Ride - the BMW R1200GS
item.description:
A Winner [Updated]

I had a 2017 BMW R1200GS in “Frozen Dark Blue Metallic” on order with a variety of gizmos including BMW’s Navigator V GPS system, keyless ride, and the Akrapovic exhaust. Being only 172cm tall, I was getting the bike lowered to better fit my height.

My …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: Platform and Data Operations Analyst
item.description: In what was more than a bit of a surprise, I was “laterally promoted” to a new role with my employer, something I only found out less than 24 hours before it was announced to the department. I shall not complain. A bit of an odd way to go about …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator
item.description: I’ve been at this Unix and Linux systems management stuff for over 20 years now. As with my undergraduate degree1, I’ve long wanted to knock out a few relevant technology certifications. Following on from my AWS SysOps certification, I took and passed the Linux Foundation’s LFCS …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: My First Pull Request
item.description:
I use Pelican and the pelican-bootstrap3 theme to built this site.

I made my first real pull request today in the form of a minor contribution to pelican-bootstrap3. I added a feature that allows you to add a series of images to the sidebar. The original intent was to allow …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: AWS SysOps Administration - Associate
item.description: Last week I took the AWS SysOps Administrator - Associate test and passed with flying colours. Next step will be the Developer and then the DevOps certifications, both of which I hope to complete this year. I look forward to doing a lot more work in AWS in the future.
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond

item.title: My First Kaggle Competition
item.description:
We are wrapping up the final week of MIT’s 15.071x “The Analytics Edge” course, delivered online via edX.org.

As part of the course I participated in my first Kaggle competition. I finished in 1406th place out of 2923 people with a Private leaderboard score of 0.88319 …
item.dc:creator: Mark Drummond
